folio.label: 145r
folio.l: And thurgú thy dronke nose , semetú the soun
As thougú thow seidest ay , Sampsou~ Sampsou~
And ©et w w god woot , Sampsou~ drank neuäe no wyn
Thow fallest , as it were , a stiked Swyn
Thy tonge is lost , and al thyn honest cure
For dronkenesse , is verray sepulture
Of ma~nys wyt , and his discreciou~
In whom that drynke , hatú dominaciou~
He kan no counseil kepe , it is no drede
Now kepe ©ow fro the white , and fro the rede
And namely , fro the white wyn of lepe
That is to selle , in Fyssústrete or in Chepe
This wyn of Spaigne , crepetú subtelly
In othere wynes , growyngö faste by
Of whicú there risetú , swicú fumositee
That whan a man , hatú dronken draugútes three
And wenetú that he be , at hom in Chepe
He is in spaigne , rigút at the Toun of lepe
Nat at the Rochel , ne at Burdeux Toun
And tha~ne wol he seye , Sampsou~ Sampsou~
¢ But herknetú lordynges , o word I ©ow preye
That alle the souereyne actes , dar I seye
Of victories , in the olde testament
Thurgú verray god , that is omnipotent´
Were don in abstinence , and in preyere
Loketú the Bible , and there ©e moun it lere
Loke Attilla , the grete conquerour
Deyede in his slep~ , witú shame & dishonour
Bledyngö ay at his nose , in dronkenesse
A Capitayn shulde lyue , in sobrenesse
And ouä al this , a vysetú ©ow rigút wel
What was comaunded , vn to lamwel
Nat Samuel , but lamwel sey I
Redetú the Bible , and fynde it expresly
Of wyn ©euyngö , to hem that han Iustise
Namore of this , for it may wel suffise
¢ And now that I haue spoken , of Glotonye
Now wol I ©ow defenden , hasardrye
Hasard , is verray moder of lesynges
And of deceyte , and cursed forswerynges
Blasphemynge of Crist , manslaugútre and wast also
Of catel and of tyme , and forther moo
It is repreue , and contrarie of honour
For to ben holde , a comune hasardour
And euäe the heyer , he is of estat
